item.title: Indian Rupee Leads Asia Gains After RBI Cracks Down on Speculation
item.description: The Indian rupee has become Asia’s best-performing currency since the central bank cracked down on speculation late last month, with most positions unwound ahead of Friday’s deadline, traders said.

item.title: China Reminds Battery Makers to Resist Excessive Capacity Growth
item.description: China summoned the country’s leading battery makers for a second time in a little over three months, reinforcing a call to restrict capacity expansion and avoid the price wars that have damaged other renewable-energy industries in the past.

item.title: Credit Fund Manager Says Africa Looks Safer as Global Wars Rise
item.description: Wars in the Middle East and Ukraine are reshaping investor risk perceptions, highlighting Africa as more resilient than its reputation suggests, according to London-based credit fund manager TLG Capital Ltd.

item.title: Stealth Alibaba Video AI Model Tops Global Ranking on Debut
item.description: A mysterious video generation model that swept to the top of global benchmarks was developed by a team under Alibaba Group Holding Ltd., sending ripples across China’s AI industry.

item.title: China Battery Storage Maker Sees Profit Boost from Iran Upheaval
item.description: A top Chinese battery storage manufacturer said it expects a sharp rise in first-quarter profit as overseas demand surges, a sign the country’s clean tech exporters stand to benefit from energy disruptions caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: Nvidia-Backed Lumentum Sees AI Filling Orders Through 2028
item.description: Lumentum Holdings Inc. said demand from US hyperscalers for its optical components is accelerating and on track to fill its order book through 2028.

item.title: Oaktree Says Exposure to Software Firms, Direct Lending Is Small
item.description: Oaktree Capital Management assured clients its exposure to software companies and direct lending remains limited, as global private credit funds retreat from areas showing heightened risk.

item.title: Japanese Tankers Head Closer To Hormuz as More Ships Gather
item.description: Two Japanese crude tankers are heading east along the Persian Gulf in the direction of the Strait of Hormuz, adding to a growing number of vessels gathered at the entrance to the waterway during a US-Iran ceasefire, even as President Donald Trump ramps up criticism and transits remain limited.

item.title: Global Rate Path Veers Higher in Wake of Another Trump Shock
item.description: Donald Trump’s second big shock to the world economy since he returned to the White House looks increasingly likely to force central banks into a new round of tightening.

item.title: China Allows State Oil Firms to Tap Their Reserves as War Drags
item.description: The Chinese government has given state refiners the green light to tap commercial reserves of oil, according to people familiar with the matter, as a six-week war in the Middle East creates a global energy crunch.

item.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Set for Best Weekly Gain Since 2020 Before Iran Talks
item.description: Emerging-market stocks are on track for their biggest weekly gain in close to six years ahead of US-Iran talks this weekend, with investors hopeful of further easing in Middle East tensions amid a shaky ceasefire.

item.title: Thai Finance Chief Sees Oil Prices Elevated for Two Years
item.description: Thailand expects oil prices to remain elevated for up to two years due to the Middle East conflict, its finance minister said, signaling prolonged pressure on a net energy importer already grappling with rising costs and slowing growth.

item.title: Skepticism Runs High as Indian Stocks Stage Swift Rebound in April
item.description: BofA analysts say Nifty still expensive relative to EM peers.

item.title: China Exits Factory Deflation After Oil Shock Boosted Prices
item.description: China exited factory deflation after more than three years, as energy costs surged when the war in Iran disrupted swaths of global oil supply.

item.title: Biggest Currency Clampdown in Decade Risks Backfiring for India
item.description: India’s boldest effort in a decade to support the rupee runs the risk of pushing away the global investors it’s spent years wooing.

item.title: China’s Money Market Signals Cash Glut, Weak Loan Demand
item.description: A rare dislocation in China’s money markets is pointing to a buildup of liquidity, which analysts say is a sign of slowing credit growth.

item.title: BOK Stands Pat as Iran War Creates Risks for Prices, Growth
item.description: South Korea’s central bank kept its benchmark rate unchanged, signaling a cautious stance as a surge in energy prices spurred by the Iran war complicates the outlook by inflaming inflation risks and threatening growth.

item.title: Restructured Chinese Carrier Hainan Air Mulls Bond Market Return
item.description: Hainan Airlines Holding Co. is looking to return to the bond market after going through a debt restructuring more than four years ago, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: CATL Short Sellers at Risk of Squeeze on Earnings Surprise
item.description: An earnings beat by Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. next week may set the stage for a short squeeze, after a surge in the battery maker’s shares on bets tied to soaring energy prices.

item.title: Elon Musk’s xAI Sues Colorado Over AI Anti-Discrimination Law
item.description: Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company xAI sued Colorado to block a new state law requiring tech companies to establish safeguards to prevent discrimination by autonomous tools in certain employment decisions, among others.

item.title: Uniqlo Owner Hits Record on Positive Global Profit Outlook
item.description: Fast Retailing Co. shares rose to a record after the Japanese apparel retailer raised its full-year operating profit outlook, thanks to strong margins and gains driven by strong Uniqlo demand in the US and Europe.

item.title: Alibaba Leads $300 Million Bet on AI Video Generator ShengShu
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd.’s cloud division led a 2 billion yuan ($293 million) funding round for ShengShu Technology, bolstering the war chest of a young contender in China’s crowded AI video contest.

item.title: Asia Economic Growth to Slow Even If Oil Stabilizes, ADB Warns
item.description: Economic growth across Asia will likely slow even if oil prices stabilize in the coming months, as the impact of war in the Middle East ripples through industries from manufacturing to tourism, according to the Asian Development Bank.

item.title: Geely’s World-Beating Stock Rally Intensifies Rivalry With BYD
item.description: Geely Automobile Holdings Ltd. is now the world’s best-performing electric-vehicle stock, following a torrid rally fueled by optimism about the reinvention of a combustion engine-era brand.

item.title: China’s Huatai Is Seeking to Start Securities Business in Japan
item.description: Huatai Securities Co., one of China’s largest brokerages, is preparing to start a securities business in Japan as a revival in the country’s financial markets draws firms from around the world.

item.title: Apple Will Close Its First Unionized Retail Location in the US
item.description: Apple Inc. is closing its retail store in Towson, Maryland — a location that drew national attention in recent years as the center of a rare unionization effort by the iPhone maker’s employees.

item.title: Colombian Inflation Quickens, Raising Odds of Further Rate Rises
item.description: Colombian inflation accelerated more than expected to its fastest pace since 2024, boosting the likelihood that the central bank will continue raising interest rates this month.

item.title: Peru Leaves Interest Rate at 4.25% as Inflation Tops Target Band
item.description: Peru left interest rates unchanged as policymakers judge that last month’s surge in inflation will prove to be temporary.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Prospects for Iran War Ceasefire
item.description: Gold steadied after three days of gains, with US President Donald Trump saying he is “optimistic” about a deal with Iran despite the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil Opens Higher With Focus on US-Iran Ceasefire: Markets Wrap
item.description: Oil opened higher Friday and US stock futures wavered as Israeli strikes in Lebanon and the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz damped optimism over a ceasefire to the Iran war.
